summary.title: Auto-sync collaboration awareness views
summary.what: Added default auto-sync coverage for status, context, and list-proposals so collaboration awareness commands refresh through the existing freshness window before returning potentially stale team state. Added an explicit log --sync flag that lets reviewer-oriented log scans request the same sync behavior without making normal history browsing slower.
summary.why: Dogfood showed that stale read surfaces can hide newly proposed work. list-proposals and context are decision inputs for humans and agents, and status is the first place users check current coordination state. log still has a heavier history-browsing use case, so freshness is opt-in there.
summary.user_goal: Use mainline workflow to make collaboration view commands refresh team state while keeping log sync explicit.

decisions.chose: Add status, context, and list-proposals to the existing auto-sync map
decisions.rationale: Stale output from these commands hides active team work or gives agents stale context. They already share the central freshness window and --no-sync escape hatch.

decisions.chose: Add log --sync instead of syncing log by default
decisions.rationale: Log is both a reviewer scan and a history browser. The explicit flag supports fresh reviewer scans without adding network latency to every history read.

decisions.chose: Route log --sync through shouldAutoSync and maybeAutoSync
decisions.rationale: Reusing the existing wrapper preserves freshness-window, --no-sync, and non-fatal network-failure behavior without duplicating sync code in the log command.

rejected.alternative: Make every read command sync by default
rejected.reason: That would make simple history inspection pay remote fetch cost even when freshness is not needed.

rejected.alternative: Implement a separate sync path inside log
rejected.reason: Duplicating the wrapper would risk diverging behavior for freshness and error handling.

risks: status, context, and list-proposals may now perform a network fetch after the freshness window expires, adding latency to those commands.
log --sync depends on the package-level logSync flag being registered before command execution; current cobra init ordering supports this.
followups: Consider flag-aware auto-sync policy if log --status proposed becomes the common reviewer entrypoint.
Consider exposing local-head versus synced-main-head separately in status to remove remaining main_head ambiguity.

architectural_claims: maybeAutoSync remains the single CLI wrapper for freshness-window sync behavior.
Collaboration awareness commands should not return stale team state after the configured freshness window.
Log freshness is opt-in through a CLI flag rather than default behavior.
behavioral_changes: mainline status auto-syncs before rendering when the last sync is stale by the configured freshness window.
mainline context auto-syncs before rendering when the last sync is stale by the configured freshness window.
mainline list-proposals auto-syncs before rendering when the last sync is stale by the configured freshness window.
mainline log --sync syncs before rendering, while mainline log without --sync keeps existing cached behavior.

api_changes.signature: mainline status/context/list-proposals auto-sync when stale unless --no-sync is provided

security_implications: More commands may fetch remote refs automatically after the freshness window, within the existing git remote trust boundary.
